replication_study.hypothesis: Among Asian subjects, making ethnicity salient (givenprimingquestionnaire=1) reduces the latent log of the minimum continuously compounded weekly interest rate required to defer payment (interval-censored outcome bounded by lndiscratelo and lndiscratehi), controlling for stakes (largestakes), time horizon (longterm), and their interaction (largelong).
replication_study.study_type: Experimental (computational reproduction on re-coded experimental dataset)

data_plan.source_type: Laboratory experiment-derived dataset (re-coded/processed from an external CSV by replicators)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Experiment 1; time-preference tasks; analysis restricted to Asian subjects (asian==1)
data_plan.sample_size: Rows: 1688 total (long format by stakes × horizon). Subject clusters and Asian-only subset counts will be reported by the analysis script at runtime.
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Task-type per subject (2 stakes × 2 horizons per subject); inference clustered at subject id.
data_plan.access_details: Provided in this package; no special access restrictions. Place the data file at /app/data/REPExperiment1Data.dta prior to execution.

qualification.explanation: This dataset contains the variables needed to estimate the focal effect using the same experimental design and variable definitions as the original study. It enables a high-fidelity computational reproduction of the focal test (interval regression of latent log interest rates) on a re-coded dataset closely mirroring the original construct. While it likely draws from the same underlying study population/instrument, it is not a new independent sample; thus, it primarily supports computational replication.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Variable constructs and analysis model match the original Experiment 1: interval regression on log reservation weekly interest rate bounds with ethnicity-salience treatment and task controls. See ReadMe.pdf (data/original/31/input/replication_data/ReadMe.pdf) for variable definitions (e.g., lndiscratelo/lndiscratehi for time preferences; treatE/givenprimingquestionnaire for treatment; largestakes and longterm). The Stata analysis specification is consistent with the focal model: ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do and RepTable2.do/RepTable2BothRounds.do (data/original/31/input/replication_data/*.do) show intreg lndiscratelo lndiscratehi givenprimingquestionnaire largestakes longterm largelong if asian==1, cluster(id). The original-study context and claim are corroborated by post_registration.json (Experiment 1; interval regression of log reservation interest rate; treatment indicator for identity salience).
qualification.deviation_from_original: The dataset identifier and provenance differ: this replication uses REPExperiment1Data.dta created from BasicStatsBenjaminStataFull.csv (as referenced in ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do), not the original Experiment1_Variables.dta mentioned in ReadMe.pdf. This suggests a re-coded dataset assembled by replicators rather than the authors’ processed file. Also, additional files for Round 2 and Both Rounds exist (REPExperiment1DataR2.dta, REPExperiment1DataBothRounds.dta), which are not referenced in the original ReadMe.pdf but appear in the provided replication_data folder. These deviations indicate the present dataset is not the authors’ distributed processed file and may have different subject counts or formatting, providing an opportunity for computational replication but not an independent new-sample replication. Citations: ReadMe.pdf (data/original/31/input/replication_data/ReadMe.pdf); ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do (data/original/31/input/replication_data/ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do); post_registration.json (data/original/31/input/post_registration.json).

data_plan.notes: Data are in long format with four rows per subject for time-preference tasks (2 stakes × 2 horizons). Required variables identified in REPExperiment1Data.dta include: asian, id, givenprimingquestionnaire, lndiscratelo, lndiscratehi, largestakes, longterm, and largelong. Paths in the original .do scripts point to local user directories and must be ignored. All IO for the Python script is hardcoded to /app/data. If using alternative datasets (REPExperiment1DataR2.dta or REPExperiment1DataBothRounds.dta), file paths must be adjusted in code accordingly.

steps: The script loads the dataset, filters to asian==1, and fits an interval regression of (lndiscratelo, lndiscratehi) on givenprimingquestionnaire, largestakes, longterm, and largelong with cluster-robust SEs clustered by id.
The script writes JSON and CSV summaries with coefficients, SEs, and p-values to /app/data and a simple log file.
Compare the treatment coefficient sign, magnitude, and significance to the original reported result.
planned_method.models: Interval regression (normal-errors Tobit type-2) implemented via statsmodels GenericLikelihoodModel with cluster-robust covariance (clusters=id).
planned_method.outcome_variable: Latent log of minimum continuously compounded weekly interest rate to defer payment, bounded by lndiscratelo (lower) and lndiscratehi (upper).
planned_method.independent_variables: givenprimingquestionnaire (ethnicity salient = 1), largestakes (indicator), longterm (indicator), largelong (largestakes × longterm).
planned_method.control_variables: largestakes, longterm, and their interaction largelong (as in the original Stata model).
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10; pandas for data IO; statsmodels for estimation; scipy for CDF/PDF.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Coefficient on givenprimingquestionnaire in the interval regression (Asian subjects only).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Two-sided z-test/t-test using cluster-robust standard errors clustered by id; primary interest in negative direction consistent with hypothesis.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion at the row level for observations with both bounds missing; exact, left-, right-, and interval-censored observations are handled natively by the likelihood.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not applicable; focal single primary coefficient.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Primary criterion: coefficient on givenprimingquestionnaire significantly negative at conventional thresholds (p<0.05), with original benchmark p<0.01 reported in initial_details.txt; report exact p-values and cluster-robust SEs.

files.replicate_experiment1_interval_regressio: Entry-point Python script placed in replication_data/. Loads /app/data/REPExperiment1Data.dta; filters to asian==1; fits interval regression of (lndiscratelo, lndiscratehi) on givenprimingquestionnaire, largestakes, longterm, and largelong with cluster-robust SEs by id; writes results to /app/data/replication_results_experiment1_asian.json, /app/data/replication_results_experiment1_asian.csv, and /app/data/replication_log.txt.

codebase.notes: This Python rewrite replaces the Stata intreg calls shown in ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do and RepTable2.do, complying with the requirement that all analysis be run in Python and all IO be under /app/data. The custom IntervalRegression class matches Stata’s interval regression likelihood (normal errors) and uses statsmodels’ GenericLikelihoodModel with robust clustering.

analysis.instructions: 1) Ensure REPExperiment1Data.dta is available at /app/data. 2) Execute: python replication_data/replicate_experiment1_interval_regression__py.py. 3) Inspect /app/data/replication_results_experiment1_asian.json and CSV for coefficients, SEs, and p-values. 4) Confirm the sign and magnitude of the treatment coefficient (givenprimingquestionnaire) and its significance level.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Direction (negative), magnitude of treatment coefficient (compare to -1.4165), cluster-robust SE (compare to ~0.3783), and p-value/significance threshold (1% or 5%). Report absolute and percentage differences from the original benchmark.
